creator: Joe Chu
source_text:
⇩ Full Recipe 🥗 ⇩

Macros per 1 serving:
Protein: 30g
Carbs: 70g
Fat: 20g
Calories: 570

Ingredients per 1 serving:
- 4 sheets of rice paper
- 4 oz ground pork, raw
- 1 shiitake mushroom, whole
- 1/2 shallot, whole
- 1 green onion, whole, medium
- 1/2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1/2 tbsp phu quoc fish sauce
- salt and pepper, to taste
- 1 second spray oil

[Spicy Fish Sauce]
- 1.5 tbsp lime juice
- 1 tbsp garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 red chili pepper (Thai chili)
- 2 tbsp phu quoc fish sauce

[Toppings]
- 3 tbsp cilantro, diced and whole
- 1/2 tbsp fried shallots
- 1 red chili pepper (Thai chili)

How to make it yourself:
1. Finely chop your shallot, mushroom, red chili pepper,
and green onions.
2. In a bowl, add your ground pork, diced mushrooms,
shallots, green onions, soy sauce, fish sauce, and salt
and pepper to taste and mix well until evenly combined.
3. In a small bowl, mix lime juice, minced garlic brown
sugar, red chili pepper, and fish sauce to make your
spicy fish dipping sauce.
4. In a pan on medium heat coated with spray oil, cook
your ground pork mix until cooked through. Set aside to
cool to room temperature.
5. Wet your rice paper in a bowl of water evenly, shake
off excess water, and place on a flat surface and scoop
a few spoonfuls of your cooked pork onto the center of
your rice paper.
6. Roll your rice paper into long logs/burritos, repeat
with the remaining rice paper to make more rolls, and
place the rolls on a plate.
7. Pour your sauce on top of your rice paper rolls, top
off with fried shallots, red chili peppers, and cilantro
and enjoy!

📩 Save this banh cuon recipe to make for later and if
you make it, post it and tag me in it! I’d love to see how
you liked the recipe! :)

#highprotein #lowcalorierecipes #vietnamesefood
#healthyrecipe #weightlossrecipes #banhcuon
#ricepaper

expected.name: banh cuon

In a pan on medium heat coated with spray oil, cook your ground pork mix until cooked through. Set aside to cool to room temperature.
Wet your rice paper in a bowl of water evenly, shake off excess water, and place on a flat surface and scoop a few spoonfuls of your cooked pork onto the center of your rice paper.
Roll your rice paper into long logs/burritos, repeat with the remaining rice paper to make more rolls, and place the rolls on a plate.
Pour your sauce on top of your rice paper rolls, top off with fried shallots, red chili peppers, and cilantro and enjoy!

notes: Agent-labeled from the frozen Instagram description before viewing parser output.
Accepted after the parser learned to join hard-wrapped numbered-step continuations.
